proyecto.title: The Project — Creative Ecosystem
proyecto.quote: We are not building a studio; we are developing a system that turns ideas into sonic experiences with both commercial and cultural relevance.
proyecto.desc_p1: Joe on the Sound is a Panamanian creative ecosystem integrating music, technology, Latin American culture and independent artistic development. The project seeks to build a self-sustaining structure connecting music production, digital content, emerging artists, data analysis, sonic identity and community.
proyecto.desc_p2: Founded in Panama and conceived with a global outlook (2026–2029), the project aims to position Latin American creativity in the new digital era through art, experimentation and the strategic use of technology.
proyecto.desc_ai: Artificial intelligence is presented as an advanced support tool, not as the project's creative core. AI optimizes creation, experimentation and distribution, while the central value remains entirely human: artistic vision, cultural judgment, narrative and musical sensitivity.

about.title: About Joe
about.p1: JOE is a deeply curious person who loves music and everything happening behind sound. A multi-instrumentalist, independent producer and software developer based in North Panama, Panama, his proposal grows from an unusual blend of artistic sensitivity, applied technology and constant experimentation.
about.p2: His story began in 2009, producing his first album with early versions of FL Studio, with more enthusiasm than technique and a genuine need to explore. That project was released as Música Para Las Discotecas: a production that began as a hobby, grew beyond expectations and ended much sooner than he likes to admit.
about.p3: Half because he did not believe enough in himself. Half because the local market did not seem to believe much in its own artists at the time. That period left an anecdote that still appears as a personal mark in many songs: the studio cry “¡No quiero cinta!” — roughly, “I don't want any more runaround!” — a spontaneous phrase capturing his drive to keep moving and searching until he finds something of his own.
about.p4: Over time, JOE never stopped experimenting. Music blended with software development, web architecture, audience-metric analysis and custom DSP — Digital Signal Processing — tools. This hybrid combination of engineer and artist lets him configure his physical and digital studio, automate workflows and apply Artificial Intelligence not as a substitute for creativity, but as an advanced tool for harmonic modulation, generative texture synthesis and technical metadata optimization.
about.p5: The project's core remains deeply sensitive and Latin American: capturing the pulse of Panamanian drums, shaping the timbral expression of vintage analog keyboards and writing lyrics with purpose, identity and cultural belonging for global distribution.
about.p6: Every attempt became more complex, every idea opened another door, and that entire path eventually took shape as a larger project: Joe on the Sound. The complete story is long—the kind that cannot be explained in a biography. It is better told over a glass of wine among friends.
